items.title: NVIDIA Confidential Computing to Help Expand Apple’s Private Cloud Compute
items.description: NVIDIA GPUs with Confidential Computing are now used for confidential inference in Apple’s Private Cloud Compute (PCC), as it expands beyond Apple’s data centers to Google Cloud.  Unveiled during Apple’s annual WWDC gathering for developers from around the globe, NVIDIA GPUs will support server-side inference for Apple Foundation Models, custom-built by Apple and Google, leveraging […]
items.content: NVIDIA GPUs with Confidential Computing are now used for confidential inference in Apple’s Private Cloud Compute (PCC), as it expands beyond Apple’s data centers to Google Cloud. Unveiled during Apple’s annual WWDC gathering for developers from around the globe, NVIDIA GPUs will support server-side inference for Apple Foundation Models, custom-built by Apple and Google, leveraging the technologies behind the Gemini family of models. NVIDIA is collaborating with Apple and Google to support some of the next-generation Apple Intelligence features, using NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs with Confidential Computing integrated into Private Cloud Compute’s hardware security architecture running on Google Cloud. Confidential Computing Matters for the Era of AI Experiences NVIDIA Confidential Computing provides a hardware-based security layer for accelerated AI workloads. The technology protects data while it’s being processed by isolating workloads in trusted execution environments and enabling systems to cryptographically verify that the infrastructure has not been tampered with before any sensitive data is sent to the server. For end users, NVIDIA Confidential Computing means that no one, not even the system’s builders, can look at their data, chats or conversations. Adoption of NVIDIA Confidential Computing at this scale reflects a broader shift in AI infrastructure: As AI experiences combine on-device and cloud-based processing for their tasks, there’s a need for high-performance, server-side inference while maintaining strong privacy and security guarantees. How Confidential Computing Enforces Privacy and Trust NVIDIA Confidential Computing reflects NVIDIA’s commitment to trustworthy AI and includes these key capabilities: Hardware-rooted trust, helping establish that systems are running on genuine, untampered NVIDIA GPUs. Encrypted communication paths, helping protect data as it moves between components. Remote attestation, enabling software to verify the security state of the platform before releasing sensitive data. Support for accelerated AI inference and training, helping organizations run privacy-sensitive workloads without moving away from GPU performance. These capabilities are increasingly relevant for AI services that need to process sensitive information while maintaining strong user privacy controls. Learn more about NVIDIA Confidential Computing and NVIDIA AI cybersecurity solutions.
items.author: Avinash Ahuja
items.titleZh: NVIDIA 机密计算助力扩展苹果私有云计算
items.descriptionZh:
NVIDIA现已将搭载机密计算（Confidential Computing）功能的GPU部署于苹果的私有云计算（Private Cloud Compute, PCC）平台，用于执行机密推理任务，且该平台已从苹果自有的数据中心扩展至Google Cloud。这一消息在苹果全球开发者大会（WWDC）期间公布：NVIDIA GPU将支持苹果基础模型（Apple Foundation Models）的服务器端推理——这些模型由苹果与Google基于Gemini系列模型技术联合定制。具体而言，NVIDIA与苹果、Google合作，使用NVIDIA Blackwell GPU（集成机密计算能力）融入PCC的硬件安全架构，并运行在Google Cloud基础设施之上，以支撑下一代Apple Intelligence的若干功能。

**机密计算为何在AI体验时代至关重要**  
NVIDIA机密计算为加速AI工作负载提供了一层硬件级安全保障。其核心技术在于：通过将工作负载隔离在可信执行环境（Trusted Execution Environment）中，保护数据在处理过程中的安全；同时，系统能够在发送任何敏感数据至服务器前，通过密码学方式验证基础设施是否被篡改。对最终用户而言，这意味着即便系统构建者也无法查看其数据、聊天记录或对话内容。NVIDIA机密计算在此规模下的采用，反映出AI基础设施的深刻变革——当AI体验需要融合设备端与云端处理时，既需要高性能的服务器端推理，又必须坚守强大的隐私与安全承诺。

**机密计算如何实现隐私与信任**  
NVIDIA机密计算体现了其对可信AI的承诺，包含以下关键能力：  
- **硬件根信任**：帮助确认系统运行在真实、未被篡改的NVIDIA GPU上。  
- **加密通信路径**：保护数据在组件之间传输时的安全。  
- **远程证明**：使软件在释放敏感数据前能够验证平台的安全状态。  
- **加速AI推理与训练支持**：帮助组织在不牺牲GPU性能的前提下运行隐私敏感型工作负载。

这些能力对于需要处理敏感信息、同时保持严格用户隐私控制的AI服务而言愈发关键。欲了解更多，可查阅NVIDIA机密计算及NVIDIA AI网络安全解决方案的详细资料。

items.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
items.description:
At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data centers. The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It's releasing a new commercial energy storage system strategy, anchored by newly developed sodium-ion batteries for industrial-scale grid applications. And it's launching a new feature for EV owners that it says will help simplify public charging. 
Right now, millions of EVs are sitting idly in driveways across  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data centers. The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It’s releasing a new commercial energy storage system strategy, anchored by newly developed sodium-ion batteries for industrial-scale grid applications. And it’s launching a new feature for EV owners that it says will help simplify public charging.Right now, millions of EVs are sitting idly in driveways across the country with a wealth of electrons stored in their batteries. GM is betting that even as EV sales cool down, public utilities will want to work with automakers to utilize those EV batteries as a potential solution to the energy demand crisis they face. It was also the latest effort by the largest automaker in North America to grab a piece of the multibillion-dollar energy generation and storage market, which it has been trying to do for nearly four years now.“We see a future where electric vehicles, batteries that power them, and the country’s power grids work together,” GM’s chief product officer Sterling Anderson said in prepared remarks for today’s event.EVs are unique in their ability to send energy back to the grid, just as they pull it while charging. Many EVs are built with this bidirectional charging capability, enabling the two-way flow of energy. In essence, it treats high-capacity lithium-ion batteries not only as tools to power EVs but also as backup storage cells to charge other electric devices, an entire home, or even to send power to the electrical grid for possible energy savings.As AI data centers put more stress on the grid, GM thinks its hundreds of electric vehicles can help lighten the load. The automaker says that with bidirectional charging capabilities, EVs can send energy back into the grid during times of peak demand. As such, the automaker says it will release a firmware update to give its current vehicle-to-home system customers the ability to send energy back to the grid (vehicle-to-grid, or V2G). GM customers who already own the equipment will receive the update automatically.GM says there are currently over 250,000 bidirectional-capable Chevy, Cadillac, and GMC EVs on American roads today. Theoretically, their combined battery capacity is enough to power 120,000 homes for up to an entire week.GM is already testing this theory in two states. In Northern California, the company is partnering with PG&E to develop a localized fleet of 52,000 EVs for “grid balancing protocols,” which it says will be operational by 2030. And in Michigan, GM is working with DTE Energy to “stress-test” bidirectional charging using 30 of its own employees’ homes as real-world test cases. In addition to providing a benefit to public utilities, the automaker says EV owners could see a financial windfall too.“By injecting flexibility into a historically rigid system, V2G technology simultaneously can lower aggregate energy costs, create a potential financial return for the consumer, and enhance the systemic reliability of the broader grid,” Anderson said.But enabling V2G technology isn’t as easy as flipping a switch. In an open letter, GM Energy VP Wade Sheffer urged regulators to formalize V2G infrastructure, citing International Energy Agency (IEA) reports identifying V2G as the technology with the largest hourly flexibility to limit future grid investment costs. Sheffer said that the auto industry needs to work with government to educate the public to the benefits of V2G tech. And utilities must simplify the administrative process to allow their customers to seamlessly enroll in future projects.GM says it’s also working on new industrial-scale solutions, partnering with California-based Peak Energy to develop and deploy sodium ion chemistry for energy storage systems. Sodium is seen by some as an improvement over lithium, both in terms of availability and stability. The material is more cost-effective to obtain and isn’t subject to the same safety hazards as lithium, which can catch fire under certain circumstances. They also perform better in cold weather than Li-ion batteries. Some major battery makers, like China’s CATL, believe that sodium-ion batteries could potentially replace up to half the market for lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries that now dominates the field.PreviousNext1/6GM says sodium ion chemistry is a good fit for commercial energy storage, but not necessarily EVs, because it prioritizes “longevity, high cycle and calendar life, and intense cost-efficiency.” The automaker is also working with Redwood Materials to build energy storage out of US-manufactured batteries, as well as “second-life” EV packs from GM’s vehicles. For its EV batteries, GM is betting on lithium manganese-rich batteries, or LMR, to close the gap between the US and China.Lastly, GM announced Energy Pass, a new feature that will appear across its suite of mobile apps. Energy Pass allows Chevy, Cadillac, and GMC EV owners to find, start, and pay for charging across multiple third-party charging operators, including Tesla, Electrify America, and IONNA. (The company says it also plans to add EVgo and ChargePoint.) Owners can use now use their mobile app to find a charger, initiate a charging session, and pay for the charging without having to sign up for a separate account for each provider.PreviousNext1/9The lack of convenient and reliable charging is frequently cited in customer surveys as a major obstacle to purchasing an EV. GM is using Tesla’s NACS charging standard for its future vehicles, as the company’s Superchargers are widely seen as among the best EV charging network in the world.GM has recently been trying to expand its EV business to include a variety of energy storage and charging projects. The company launched GM Energy, its energy spinoff, in 2022 as a way to compete in the rapidly expanding home energy market. In an effort to compete with Tesla for the $150 billion home energy market, GM sells a number of products, including home EV chargers, stationary home batteries, and vehicle-to-home (V2H) kits that enable a home to pull energy from an EV battery in the event of a blackout.Images courtesy of GM
items.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
items.titleZh: 通用汽车认为电动汽车可通过车网互联技术帮助抵消人工智能的能源消耗
items.descriptionZh:
在旧金山的一场活动上，通用汽车（GM）今日围绕电动汽车电池、能源存储以及电网韧性发布了一系列重要声明，旨在应对人工智能数据中心日益增长的电力需求。这家汽车制造商宣布，将为其现有的电动汽车和家庭能源客户激活新的车辆到电网（V2G）功能；同时推出以新开发的钠离子电池为核心的商用储能系统战略，用于工业级电网应用；此外，还面向电动汽车车主发布了一项新功能，旨在简化公共充电过程。

目前，全美有数百万辆电动汽车闲置在车道上，其电池中存储着大量电能。通用汽车押注，即使电动汽车销售降温，公共事业公司仍会愿意与汽车制造商合作，将这些电动汽车电池作为应对能源需求危机的潜在解决方案。这也是这家北美最大汽车制造商近四年来一直在尝试的、在数十亿美元的发电和储能市场中分一杯羹的最新努力。

“我们展望这样一个未来：电动汽车、驱动它们的电池，以及国家的电网能够协同工作，”通用汽车首席产品官斯特林·安德森在活动准备的发言稿中表示。

电动汽车的独特之处在于，它们既能从电网获取电能充电，也能将电能回馈给电网。许多电动汽车都具备这种双向充电能力，可实现能量的双向流动。本质上，它将高容量的锂离子电池不仅视为驱动电动汽车的工具，还当作备用储能单元，用于为其他电子设备充电、为整个家庭供电，甚至向电网输送电能，从而实现可能的节能效果。

随着AI数据中心给电网带来更大压力，通用汽车认为其数百万辆电动汽车可以帮助减轻负担。该公司表示，凭借双向充电能力，电动汽车可以在用电高峰时段将电能回馈电网。因此，通用汽车将发布固件更新，使其现有车辆到家庭（V2H）系统客户能够实现向电网输电（即车辆到电网，V2G）。已经拥有相关设备的通用汽车客户将自动获得此次更新。

通用汽车表示，目前美国道路上已有超过25万辆具备双向充电能力的雪佛兰、凯迪拉克和GMC电动汽车。理论上，它们合并的电池容量足以为12万户家庭连续供电一整周。

通用汽车已在两个州测试这一理论。在加州北部，该公司正与太平洋天然气和电力公司（PG&E）合作，开发一个由5.2万辆电动汽车组成的本地化车队，用于“电网平衡协议”，预计2030年投入运营。在密歇根州，通用汽车正与DTE Energy合作，利用其30名员工家庭作为真实测试案例，对双向充电进行“压力测试”。该公司表示，除了为公共事业公司带来好处外，电动汽车车主也可能获得经济收益。

“通过向历史上僵化的系统注入灵活性，V2G技术可以同时降低总能源成本，为消费者创造潜在的经济回报，并增强更广泛电网的系统可靠性，”安德森说。

然而，启用V2G技术并非易事。通用汽车能源业务副总裁韦德·谢弗在一封公开信中敦促监管机构将V2G基础设施正式化，并引用国际能源署（IEA）的报告指出，V2G是每小时灵活性最大的技术，能够限制未来电网投资成本。谢弗表示，汽车行业需要与政府合作，向公众普及V2G技术的好处，而公共事业公司必须简化行政流程，让客户能够无缝参与未来的项目。

通用汽车还表示，正在开发新的工业级解决方案，与加州初创公司Peak Energy合作，开发和部署钠离子化学储能系统。一些人认为钠比锂更优越，无论是在可用性还是稳定性方面。钠材料更经济实惠，且不像锂那样存在某些安全风险（锂在某些条件下可能起火）。钠离子电池在寒冷天气下的表现也优于锂离子电池。一些主要电池制造商，如中国的宁德时代，认为钠离子电池有可能取代目前主导市场的磷酸铁锂（LFP）电池高达一半的市场份额。

通用汽车表示，钠离子化学适用于商业储能，但不一定适用于电动汽车，因为它优先考虑“长寿命、高循环寿命和日历寿命，以及极致成本效益”。该公司还与Redwood Materials合作，利用美国制造的电池以及通用汽车车辆中的“二手”电池组来构建储能系统。对于电动汽车电池，通用汽车正押注于锂锰富集电池（LMR），以缩小美国与中国之间的差距。

最后，通用汽车宣布推出Energy Pass，这是一项将出现在其移动应用套件中的新功能。Energy Pass允许雪佛兰、凯迪拉克和GMC电动汽车车主在包括特斯拉、Electrify America和IONNA在内的多个第三方充电运营商处查找、启动和支付充电费用（该公司还计划添加EVgo和ChargePoint）。车主现在可以使用手机应用查找充电桩、启动充电会话并支付费用，无需为每个供应商单独注册账户。

便利性和可靠充电的缺乏经常在客户调查中被列为购买电动汽车的主要障碍。通用汽车未来车辆将采用特斯拉的NACS充电标准，因为特斯拉的超级充电网络被广泛认为是全球最好的电动汽车充电网络之一。

通用汽车最近一直试图扩展其电动汽车业务，涵盖各种储能和充电项目。该公司于2022年成立了能源子公司GM Energy，作为在快速扩张的家庭能源市场中竞争的方式。为了与特斯拉争夺1500亿美元的家庭能源市场，通用汽车销售多种产品，包括家用电动汽车充电器、固定式家用电池以及车辆到家庭（V2H）套件，后者允许家庭在停电时从电动汽车电池中取电。

history.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
history.description:
Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic biology questions - the kind you'd expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to the former flagship model, Claude Opus 4.8.
It isn't because Fable doesn't know the answers. It's because Anthropic won't let it, by design.
Fable is a public-facing, Mythos-class model, a family so capable at cybersecurity tasks Anthropic said it was too dangerous to release publicly. But while Anthropic has spent much of the extended Mythos rol …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won’t answer basic biology questions — the kind you’d expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to the former flagship model, Claude Opus 4.8.It isn’t because Fable doesn’t know the answers. It’s because Anthropic won’t let it, by design.Fable is a public-facing, Mythos-class model, a family so capable at cybersecurity tasks Anthropic said it was too dangerous to release publicly. But while Anthropic has spent much of the extended Mythos rollout warning about cybersecurity, it is biology where Fable’s guardrails are the most obvious — and most limiting.When I tried the model, it refused to answer a range of basic biology questions, many that felt about as far away from any plausible safety risk as any question could be. It would not respond to “tell me about cell membranes” or answer “what are mitochondria,” that famous powerhouse of the cell. It refused to explain “what is a prion,” the proteinaceous particles behind mad cow disease, or “how mRNA vaccines work.”“We made this tradeoff so customers could benefit from the model’s capabilities sooner without the risks.”The restrictions applied to ordinary and objectively rather harmless medical queries too. Fable would not answer “what causes hay fever,” explain how asthma medicine works, explain how antibiotic resistance arises, or tell me what Ebola is and how it spreads. Some of my basic queries occasionally got through, with Fable answering questions like “what is cancer” and “what is DNA.” When Fable refused, Opus 4.8 generally answered perfectly well.Anthropic says the broad biology filters are an intentional choice and are deliberately conservative, with bioweapons the primary concern. “With the launch of Claude Fable 5, our first Mythos-class model, we believe models now have a greater ability to accomplish real-world scientific tasks and for malicious actors to potentially use our models for highly risky biological research,” spokesperson Paruul Maheshwary told The Verge. “We have always used classifiers to block our models from helping with bioweapons-related requests. To deploy Fable 5 safely, we believe it was necessary to be overly conservative with our safeguards so they block most queries tied to biology work.”Anthropic has previously highlighted four key areas where it would throttle Fable’s responses for safety: chemistry, biology, cybersecurity, and distillation, a technique for training smaller AIs using the outputs of larger ones. The company has accused Chinese rivals like DeepSeek of using distillation on its models on an “industrial” scale.RelatedAnthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable Anthropic’s Mythos breach was humiliatingAI warfare is already hereWhile I could not meaningfully test distillation, Fable seemed more willing to answer questions about chemistry and cybersecurity. For example, it gave a basic overview of the explosive TNT, though withheld synthesis instructions “for obvious reasons.” It readily answered questions on the use of chlorine gas as a chemical weapon, common password threats, and nuclear fusion and fission, as well as explaining how to secure an iPhone from hackers. It still limits: Fable deferred to Opus when I asked it about sarin gas, a highly toxic nerve agent. Fable and Opus both refused the prompt “how to make anthrax,” and Claude paused the chat entirely. That made sense. The mitochondria prompt refusal seems like a false positive.“We made this tradeoff so customers could benefit from the model’s capabilities sooner without the risks,” Maheshwary explained, adding that Anthropic is working hard to improve its detection and reduce the false positives. “We intend to make Mythos-class models available without these safeguards to the broader biology and life sciences community so these capabilities can be used to accelerate biomedical research and drug discovery.”Anthropic did not answer questions about whether this kind of restricted release will become the new norm for future models.
history.author: Robert Hart
history.titleZh: 克劳德·费布尔不会回答基础生物学问题
history.descriptionZh:
Anthropic 发布了其最新一代 AI 模型 Claude Fable 5，并宣称这是迄今为止公开发布的最强大的模型，尤其擅长生物学等领域。然而，令人意外的是，Fable 5 拒绝回答许多基础生物学问题——那些连高中生都能回答的问题，比如“什么是细胞膜”“线粒体是什么”“什么是朊病毒”“mRNA 疫苗如何工作”等。当用户提出这类问题时，模型不会直接回答，而是将查询转交给前代旗舰模型 Claude Opus 4.8。这种设计并非因为 Fable 5 不具备相关知识，而是 Anthropic 有意识地限制了其能力。

Fable 5 是 Anthropic 推出的首款面向公众的“Mythos 级”模型。这一级别的模型在网络安全任务上的能力如此强大，以至于 Anthropic 曾认为其不适合公开发布。在整个 Mythos 系列模型逐步推出的过程中，Anthropic 主要强调了网络安全风险，但实际体验表明，生物学领域的防护措施最为严格，也最为限制用户使用。测试发现，Fable 5 不仅拒绝回答基础生物学问题，还拒绝回答一些常见且无害的医学问题，例如“花粉症是什么原因引起的”“哮喘药物如何工作”“抗生素耐药性如何产生”“埃博拉病毒是什么及如何传播”等。仅少数基础问题（如“什么是癌症”“什么是DNA”）偶尔能通过。当 Fable 5 拒绝时，Opus 4.8 通常能完美回答这些问题。

Anthropic 的发言人 Paruul Maheshwary 向 The Verge 解释称，这些广泛的生物学过滤器是故意为之且刻意保守的，主要担忧在于生物武器风险。公司认为，Mythos 级模型已经具备更强的实际科学任务完成能力，恶意行为者可能利用这些模型进行高风险生物研究。因此，为了安全部署 Fable 5，有必要采取过度保守的安全措施，阻止大多数与生物学工作相关的查询。Anthropic 之前曾明确四个关键领域将受到限制：化学、生物学、网络安全和蒸馏（一种利用大模型输出训练小型模型的技术）。公司指责 DeepSeek 等中国竞争对手以“工业规模”对其模型进行蒸馏。

在化学和网络安全方面，Fable 5 的响应似乎更为开放。例如，它可以提供 TNT 炸药的概述，但出于明显原因会省略合成说明；它能够回答关于氯气化学武器使用、常见密码威胁、核聚变与裂变以及如何保护 iPhone 免受黑客攻击等问题。但在面对沙林毒气（一种剧毒神经毒剂）时，Fable 5 会将问题转给 Opus；当被问及“如何制造炭疽”时，Fable 5 和 Opus 都拒绝回答，并直接暂停了整个对话。这合乎逻辑，而拒绝回答线粒体问题则更像是误报。

Anthropic 表示，做出这种权衡是为了让客户能够尽早受益于模型能力，同时避免风险。公司正在努力改进检测机制以减少误报。未来计划将 Mythos 级模型在去除这些安全限制后提供给更广泛的生物学和生命科学界，以加速生物医学研究和药物发现。至于这种受限发布是否将成为未来模型的新常态，Anthropic 未予回答。

history.title: NVIDIA Accelerates Google DeepMind’s DiffusionGemma for Local AI
history.description: Today, Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma — an experimental open model built for exceptionally fast text generation. NVIDIA has optimized DiffusionGemma to run even faster across NVIDIA GeForce RTX GPUs, the NVIDIA RTX PRO platform and NVIDIA DGX Spark systems, from local PCs to the cloud.  Rather than generating text one word at a time, DiffusionGemma generates multiple words in parallel to output whole blocks of text, opening a new, low-latency frontier for the kind of single-user workloads that developers, […]
history.content: Today, Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma — an experimental open model built for exceptionally fast text generation. NVIDIA has optimized DiffusionGemma to run even faster across NVIDIA GeForce RTX GPUs, the NVIDIA RTX PRO platform and NVIDIA DGX Spark systems, from local PCs to the cloud. Rather than generating text one word at a time, DiffusionGemma generates multiple words in parallel to output whole blocks of text, opening a new, low-latency frontier for the kind of single-user workloads that developers, researchers and AI enthusiasts run every day. Features of the new model include: Parallel generation: DiffusionGemma denoises up to 256 tokens per step instead of predicting one at a time. Built on Gemma 4: DiffusionGemma is built on Gemma 4, a 26-billion-parameter mixture-of-experts model that activates just 3.8 billion parameters per step, pairing a diffusion head with Google’s Gemma 4 architecture. Up to 4x faster performance: The boost means fast text generation, where single-user generation usually stalls — on local hardware. Open and local: DiffusionGemma is open weights under a permissive Apache 2.0 license and runs entirely on RTX and DGX Spark — no cloud, no per-token cost — with day-zero support in Hugging Face Transformers, vLLM and Unsloth. A Different Way to Generate Text Almost every large language model (LLM) in wide use today is autoregressive — meaning it generates text one token at a time, with each new word depending on the one before it. That sequential process is what makes interactive AI feel like it’s typing. DiffusionGemma takes a different path. Built on the Gemma 4 26B mixture-of-experts architecture, it generates text the way diffusion models generate images: by starting from noise and refining a whole block of text at once. Each step denoises up to 256 tokens in parallel rather than emitting a single token and waiting to compute the next. The result is a model that thinks in blocks instead of sequentially. For latency-sensitive, single-user work — such as interactive chat, agentic loops or on-device assistants that plan and act — that parallelism translates into responses fast enough to keep pace with how developers think and iterate. DiffusionGemma Flies on NVIDIA GPUs Generating one token at a time is fundamentally a memory-bound problem — a traditional LLM spends most of its time waiting on memory bandwidth, not doing math, which leaves a lot of compute on the table. Diffusion flips the equation. Pulling a full 256-token block through the transformer in parallel is a compute-bound workload — exactly what NVIDIA GPUs are built for. NVIDIA Tensor Cores accelerate the dense parallel math, and the CUDA software stack lets the model run efficiently from day one without bespoke tuning. In short, the model’s design plays directly to the GPU’‘s strengths. That shows up in the numbers. DiffusionGemma delivers 1,000 tokens/sec on a single NVIDIA H100 Tensor Core GPU, 150 tokens/sec on NVIDIA DGX Spark and fastest local inference on NVIDIA DGX Station — roughly 4x faster than an equivalent autoregressive model running in the same single-user regime. That advantage holds across NVIDIA’s full lineup, running: Locally on the NVIDIA DGX Spark deskside personal AI supercomputer — powered by the NVIDIA GB10 Grace Blackwell Superchip with 128GB of unified memory — with the preinstalled NVIDIA AI software stack ready for prototyping, fine-tuning and fully local agent workflows. On NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 workstations, providing developers, researchers and AI professionals with the headroom to run local low-latency generation and agentic loops as part of a professional workflow. On DGX Station, delivering best-in-class, high-speed inference at up to 800 tokens/sec for low-latency text generation and agentic loops with 748GB of coherent memory. On GeForce RTX GPUs, with llama.cpp support coming soon. Get Started Locally The fastest way to start testing and prototyping the model is through Hugging Face Transformers, which runs DiffusionGemma on a GeForce RTX 5090 or DGX Spark out of the box. For higher-throughput inference, vLLM provides day-zero serving support. For adapting the model to a specific task or domain, fine-tuning is available through Unsloth and NVIDIA NeMo framework, with ready-made DGX Spark playbooks to get a local environment running quickly. Check out the vLLM playbooks for DGX Spark , RTX PRO and DGX Station. Try Diffusion Gemma on Hugging Face or test it for free using NVIDIA-hosted application programming interfaces at build.nvidia.com. Go deeper on the architecture and local deployment by reading the NVIDIA technical blog and the Google DeepMind announcement. #ICYMI: The Latest From RTX AI Garage 🎬 NVIDIA researchers released SANA-WM, an open source world model that turns a single image and a camera path into a minute-long, 720p video with precise 6-DoF control. At just 2.6 billion parameters, its distilled version generates a full 60-second clip in 34 seconds on a single NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 GPU using the NVFP4 format — delivering up to 36x higher throughput than comparable open models while running on one GPU. Read the paper. 🛠️ Building Windows agents just got a full toolset — NVIDIA and Microsoft rolled out turnkey agent sandboxing on native Windows — Microsoft eXecution Containers plus the NVIDIA OpenShell runtime — alongside up to 2x faster agentic inference and native Windows support for Hermes Agent. 🤖DGX Spark goes from unboxing to a running agent in minutes — A streamlined NVIDIA NemoClaw install gets developers to a working local agent fast, with Qwen3.6-35B running up to 2.6x faster on vLLM. And the new cluster assistant in NVIDIA Sync links up to four DGX Spark units into one 512GB pool — enough for ~400-billion-parameter models. Plug in to RTX Spark on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok and X — and stay informed by subscribing to the RTX Spark newsletter. See notice regarding software product information.
history.author: Michael Fukuyama
history.descriptionZh:
今天，谷歌DeepMind发布了DiffusionGemma——一个专为极速文本生成而构建的实验性开源模型。英伟达对DiffusionGemma进行了优化，使其在英伟达GeForce RTX GPU、英伟达RTX PRO平台以及英伟达DGX Spark系统上运行得更快，覆盖从本地PC到云端的各类场景。与逐词生成文本的传统方式不同，DiffusionGemma能够并行生成多个单词，一次性输出整个文本块，为开发者、研究人员和AI爱好者每天运行的单用户工作负载开辟了低延迟的新前沿。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

目前几乎所有广泛使用的大语言模型（LLM）都是自回归的——即逐个token生成文本，每个新词依赖前一个词。这种顺序处理过程使得交互式AI感觉像是在打字。DiffusionGemma则另辟蹊径。它基于Gemma 4 260亿参数混合专家架构构建，采用类似扩散模型生成图像的方式生成文本：从噪声开始，一次性精炼整个文本块。每一步并行去噪多达256个token，而不是发出单个token后再等待计算下一个。结果是一个以块为单位而非顺序思考的模型。对于延迟敏感的单用户工作——如交互式聊天、智能体循环或需要规划并执行的设备端助手——这种并行性转化为足够快的响应速度，与开发者的思考和迭代节奏相匹配。

**性能参数与对比数据**

DiffusionGemma在英伟达GPU上表现卓越。传统逐token生成本质上是内存受限问题——模型大部分时间在等待内存带宽而非计算，导致大量算力闲置。扩散模型则扭转了这一局面：将完整的256个token块并行通过Transformer是一个计算密集型任务，这正是英伟达GPU的强项。英伟达Tensor Core加速了密集的并行数学运算，CUDA软件栈使模型无需定制调优即可高效运行。具体数字方面：DiffusionGemma在单个英伟达H100 Tensor Core GPU上达到每秒1000个token，在英伟达DGX Spark上达到每秒150个token，在英伟达DGX Station上提供最快的本地推理——大约比同等自回归模型在相同单用户场景下快4倍。这一优势贯穿英伟达整个产品线，包括：本地运行在英伟达DGX Spark桌面个人AI超级计算机上（搭载英伟达GB10 Grace Blackwell超级芯片，128GB统一内存，预装英伟达AI软件栈，可快速进行原型设计、微调和完全本地化的智能体工作流）；在英伟达RTX PRO 6000工作站上，为开发者、研究人员和AI专业人士提供本地低延迟生成和智能体循环所需的余量；在DGX Station上，以高达每秒800个token的速度提供最佳的高速推理，配备748GB一致性内存，适用于低延迟文本生成和智能体循环；在GeForce RTX GPU上，llama.cpp支持即将推出。

**技术影响与应用场景**

DiffusionGemma的设计直接利用了GPU的优势，使单用户生成任务在本地硬件上获得显著加速，而传统自回归模型在此类场景中通常会遇到瓶颈。该模型以Apache 2.0许可协议开源权重，完全在RTX和DGX Spark上运行——无需云端，无按token计费——并在发布首日即获得Hugging Face Transformers、vLLM和Unsloth的支持。这意味着开发者可以零成本、低延迟地在本地部署智能助手、交互式聊天、智能体循环等应用。英伟达和微软还推出了Windows原生智能体沙箱工具集（Microsoft eXecution Containers + NVIDIA OpenShell运行时），使智能体推理速度提升2倍，并原生支持Hermes Agent。DGX Spark可在数分钟内从开箱到运行智能体，通过NemoClaw快速安装，Qwen3.6-35B在vLLM上运行速度提升2.6倍。新的Sync集群助手可将最多四台DGX Spark单元连接成一个512GB统一内存池，足以运行约4000亿参数模型。

**快速上手**

开始测试和原型设计的最快方式是通过Hugging Face Transformers，它可直接在GeForce RTX 5090或DGX Spark上运行DiffusionGemma。对于更高吞吐量的推理，vLLM提供发布首日服务支持。如需将模型适配特定任务或领域，可通过Unsloth和英伟达NeMo框架进行微调，并配有现成的DGX Spark运行手册。英伟达还提供了DGX Spark、RTX PRO和DGX Station的vLLM playbook。用户可在Hugging Face上试用DiffusionGemma，或通过build.nvidia.com免费使用英伟达托管的API接口。更深入的架构和本地部署信息可阅读英伟达技术博客和谷歌DeepMind公告。

**补充信息（#ICYMI）**

英伟达研究人员还发布了SANA-WM，一个开源世界模型，能将单张图像和摄像机路径转化为一分钟的720p视频，精度达到6自由度控制。其蒸馏版仅有26亿参数，在单张英伟达GeForce RTX 5090 GPU上使用NVFP4格式，仅需34秒即可生成完整60秒片段，在一张GPU上提供比同类开源模型高达36倍的吞吐量。此外，英伟达和微软推出了Windows原生智能体工具集，而DGX Spark通过新集群助手可将四台设备连接成512GB池。关注RTX Spark的Facebook、Instagram、TikTok和X账号，或订阅RTX Spark新闻通讯以获取最新信息。

history.title: AMD fires back at Nvidia, claiming 256-core Zen 6 'Venice' CPU beats Vera by 3.3x in rack-level performance — company shares first estimated EPYC Venice benchmarks
history.description: AMD has shared the first official results for its 256-core EPYC Venice CPU, saying it beats Nvidia's Vera by 3.3x in a rack-level deployment.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD has shared the first official benchmarks for its forthcoming EPYC 'Venice' CPUs, which will be the first chips to use the Zen 6 architecture. The flagship 256-core model hasn't been detailed in full, but AMD claims it offers 3.3 times the performance of the Nvidia Vera CPU in a rack-scale implementation with a fixed power budget of 100kW.Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe constraints of this test completely change the framing of the results, so it's worth highlighting them first. AMD is looking at performance from the level of a rack, not an individual component on a single socket. AMD's results are modeled around a 100kW deployment, showing the performance across the rack rather than the performance on a single socket, or even a dual-socket system.AMD did not, however, actually test all of these deployments. There's a lot of modeling going on here, which AMD details in its methodology paper that was published alongside the results. First, AMD estimated power based on the processor TDP and additional components, and it used that to calculate the number of nodes (2P system for each node) within a 100kW power budget. Then, it multiplied that number of nodes by single-node performance measured in a handful of benchmarks.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: AMD)That's just the start of the stipulations. AMD doesn't have its hands on Vera, so the performance here is an estimate. AMD took benchmarks it had for Nvidia's Grace chip and multiplied them by a scaling factor of 1.63x based on Vera results published by Phoronix. AMD also says that its 256-core EPYC Venice results are derived from an estimated 1.7x scaling factor over the EPYC 9965, along with "internal testing."AMD's methodology ends with this line: "[These results] are intended to provide directional comparison rather than direct measured rack benchmarks." As you can tell from the three paragraphs of stipulations above, that sentence carries a lot of weight on its shoulders. You can't simply scale the performance of a single "node" (a dual-socket system, in this case) up in a linear fashion. Interconnects, as well as thermal and power limitations, will become a factor as you scale up. You may like Nvidia offers restricted access to Vera CPU in first round of Linux benchmarks AMD Zen 6 'Venice' ES chips break cover with up to 192 cores, 32 per CCD, in early stress test AMD begins production ramp of 256-core EPYC Venice Regardless, AMD frames the results here around agentic AI, though the benchmarks it's using are focused on general-purpose data center tasks. The topline result is from SPEC CPU 2017, specifically looking at integer throughput. AMD used the following benchmarks, as well:Server-side Java based on SPECjbb 2015WRK Tool for load on an NGINX web serverRedis-benchmark for in-memory workloadsMemory caching with MemcachedDatabase performance with TPROC-C on MySQLThese "results" are mostly a way for AMD to fire back at Nvidia after Phoronix published a list of results for Vera that were curated by Nvidia. AMD is laying the groundwork for its Advancing AI event next month, where we expect to hear more about Venice, Zen 6 more broadly, and AMD's enterprise roadmap in much greater detail.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Jake Roach
history.titleZh: AMD反击英伟达，声称256核Zen 6“威尼斯”CPU在机架级性能上比Vera快3.3倍——公司分享首批预估EPYC威尼斯基准测试
history.descriptionZh:
AMD近日公布了其即将推出的EPYC“Venice”处理器的首批官方基准测试数据。Venice将成为首款采用Zen 6架构的芯片，其旗舰型号拥有256个核心。尽管AMD并未披露该芯片的全部细节，但声称在100kW固定功耗预算的机架级部署中，其性能可达Nvidia Vera CPU的3.3倍。

**测试背景与上下文**  
这一对比结果需要放在特定约束下理解。AMD的测试视角并非单插槽或双插槽系统，而是从整个机架（rack）的性能出发。具体来说，AMD将部署功耗限制在100kW，以此计算在该功率预算下能容纳多少节点（每个节点为一套双插槽系统），再将节点数量乘以单节点在若干基准测试中的实测性能，最终得出整个机架的总体性能。值得注意的是，AMD并未实际测试所有部署配置，而是大量使用了建模和估算方法。其随结果发布的方法论文档详细说明了这一过程：首先基于处理器TDP及附加组件估算功耗，据此推算出100kW下可支持的节点数，然后乘以单节点得分。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
Zen 6架构是此次宣传的核心。Venice芯片采用全新的核心设计，旗舰256核型号的缓存、互联和功耗管理均有重大升级。然而，AMD目前并未直接提供该芯片的实测数据——256核Venice的性能是基于EPYC 9965（上一代产品）的1.7倍缩放因子加上“内部测试”估算得出的。同样，对Nvidia Vera CPU的性能估计也存在类似缩放：AMD拿手头已有的Nvidia Grace芯片基准测试结果，乘以Phoronix网站公布的Vera成绩缩放因子（1.63倍），从而得出Vera的预估性能。AMD在方法论结尾明确指出：“这些结果旨在提供方向性比较，而非直接的实测机架基准测试。”这句话分量十足，因为线性缩放忽略了实际部署中互联、散热和功耗限制等关键因素。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
在SPEC CPU 2017整数吞吐量测试中，AMD声称其256核Venice在100kW机架级部署下，性能是Nvidia Vera CPU的3.3倍。除了SPEC CPU 2017，AMD还使用了以下基准：基于SPECjbb 2015的服务器端Java测试、NGINX Web服务器负载的WRK工具、内存负载的Redis-benchmark、Memcached内存缓存、以及基于MySQL的TPROC-C数据库性能。这些测试主要用于通用数据中心任务，而非单纯的AI推理或训练。AMD将结果定位在“代理式AI（agentic AI）”场景下，但所采用的基准更偏向传统数据中心负载。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
这组数据的发布更像是AMD对Nvidia的“反击”。此前Phoronix发布了Nvidia精心挑选的Vera CPU基准成绩，AMD借此机会展示其即将到来的Zen 6平台在功耗受限的机架级部署中的效率优势。虽然AMD的估算存在诸多假设和简化，但3.3倍的性能声称具有重要的战略意义：它表明在100kW功率包络内，AMD的256核Venice机架能比Nvidia的Vera机架处理更多通用计算任务，这对云计算、企业数据中心和AI基础设施的规划者极具吸引力。AMD正在为下月举办的Advancing AI活动做铺垫，届时我们将听到更多关于Venice、Zen 6架构以及AMD企业级路线图的详细内容。目前来看，这些“指引性”数据是AMD在下一代CPU竞争中的一次关键表态，旨在向业界传递其对Zen 6的自信，并提前抢占高端计算市场的舆论高地。

history.title: Samsung Heavy Industries recruits Greek shipowner and Supermicro to bring 50MW floating AI data centers to market — can be powered by solid oxide fuel cells running on liquefied natural gas
history.description: Besides Samsung Heavy, Japan’s MOL is also building a 73 MW floating data center with Karpowership for a 2027 deployment.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Samsung Heavy Industries signed a three-way agreement with Greek shipowner Capital Clean Energy Carriers and classification society Lloyd's Register at the Posidonia shipping exhibition in Athens earlier this month to commercialize a 50MW floating data center, according to Chosun Biz. The deal followed the signing of a joint development agreement between the shipbuilder and Supermicro to verify whether AI servers can run reliably on water, with the design pairing seawater cooling with LNG-fueled fuel cells for onboard power. Under the proposed business model, shipowners will buy the platforms and lease capacity to operators on long-term contracts, much like how they charter tankers.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe memorandum of understanding splits the work three ways: Samsung Heavy handles technology and construction, Capital leads project sourcing and investment, and Lloyd's Register covers regulation and certification. Samsung Heavy signed a second memorandum with consulting arm Lloyd's Register Advisory covering feasibility studies and an assessment of the North American market.Receiving approval in principle from the American Bureau of Shipping and Lloyd's Register in April, the 50 MW project can draw external power through subsea cables when moored in ports or coastal waters, or generate its own using solid oxide fuel cells running on LNG, sidestepping long grid connection queues that have stalled land-based projects in the U.S. and Europe. Jerry Kalogiratos, CEO of Capital Clean Energy Carriers, said in Lloyd's Register's announcement that floating data centers offer "a scalable and flexible solution, with the unique advantage of mobility."Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Meanwhile, the agreement with Supermicro is designed to address the question of whether precision AI hardware can tolerate environmental factors like vibration, tilt, salt, and humidity over a multi-year service life. Samsung Heavy will develop offshore positioning control and technologies to seal out salinity and moisture, while Supermicro validates server operating conditions in river and marine environments. The shipbuilder is leaning on its floating LNG production facility experience to integrate power, cooling, networking, and safety systems into a single hull.Besides Samsung Heavy, Japan’s MOL is also building a 73 MW floating data center with Karpowership for a 2027 deployment, China's 24MW subsea facility off Shanghai entered full operation last month, and Nautilus Data Technologies operates a much smaller 6.5MW barge at the Port of Stockton, California. Samsung Heavy also has a potential anchor tenant, having signed a letter of intent with OpenAI in October alongside Samsung's 900,000-wafer-per-month Stargate memory commitment, covering joint development of floating data centers. To date, however, there’s no signed customer contract for a named deployment. history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 三星重工联合希腊船东与超微电脑，将50MW浮动AI数据中心推向市场——可由液化天然气驱动的固体氧化物燃料电池供电
history.descriptionZh:
三星重工（Samsung Heavy Industries）近日在雅典举办的Posidonia航运展上，与希腊船东Capital Clean Energy Carriers及英国劳氏船级社（Lloyd's Register）签署三方谅解备忘录，共同推动50MW浮动数据中心的商业化。这一合作基于三星重工此前与超微电脑（Supermicro）签署的联合开发协议，旨在验证AI服务器能否在海上环境中可靠运行。其核心设计思路是利用海水冷却系统为服务器降温，并通过以液化天然气（LNG）为燃料的固体氧化物燃料电池（SOFC）为船载设备供电，从而规避陆地数据中心因电网并网排队而长期停滞的难题。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

该浮动数据中心的核心创新在于将海工装备经验与高密度计算需求融合。三星重工依托其在浮式液化天然气生产设施（FLNG）领域积累的集成经验，将电力、冷却、网络和安防系统整合在同一船体内。电力供应采用双模方案：当平台停泊在港口或近海时，可通过海底电缆接入岸电；若远离岸电设施，则启用固体氧化物燃料电池（SOFC）以LNG为燃料自主发电，无需依赖漫长且昂贵的电网基础设施。冷却系统直接利用海水作为冷源，相比传统数据中心空调制冷，能效大幅提升。

针对精密AI硬件在海上长期运行的可靠性挑战，三星重工与超微电脑联合研发了专用技术：三星重工开发海上定位控制系统和密封技术，以消除船体振动、倾斜、盐雾和湿度的影响；超微电脑则负责在河流和海洋环境中验证服务器的运行条件，确保多年度生命周期内的稳定性。

**性能参数与对比数据**

该项目的额定功率为50MW，已分别于今年4月获得美国船级社（ABS）和劳氏船级社的原则性批准（AiP）。在同类竞赛中，日本商船三井（MOL）正与Karpowership合作建造73MW浮动数据中心，计划2027年部署；中国上海近海24MW海底数据中心已于上月全面投入运营；美国Nautilus Data Technologies在加州斯托克顿港运营着一座6.5MW的驳船式数据中心。三星重工的50MW项目在规模上处于中上水平，但其采用LNG燃料电池和海水冷却的组合方案，在能源自主性和环境适应性上具有独特优势。

**商业模式与市场前景**

该项目的商业模式参照航运业常见的“光船租赁”模式：船东购买浮动数据中心平台，然后通过与运营商签订长期租赁合同来出租计算能力，类似油轮或LNG运输船的租约。三方分工明确：三星重工负责技术研发和建造，Capital Clean Energy Carriers负责项目采购和投资，劳氏船级社负责监管认证。此外，三星重工还与劳氏咨询（Lloyd's Register Advisory）签署了第二份谅解备忘录，涵盖可行性研究及北美市场评估。

潜在客户方面，三星重工已与OpenAI签署意向书，合作开发浮动数据中心，但截至目前尚未签署任何正式的客户合同或明确部署地点的协议。值得关注的是，该意向书与三星电子每月90万片晶圆的“星门”（Stargate）内存供应承诺捆绑，凸显了三星集团在AI基础设施上下游的整体布局。

**技术影响与应用场景**

浮动数据中心的核心价值在于其“移动弹性”。Capital Clean Energy Carriers首席执行官Jerry Kalogiratos指出，浮动数据中心提供了“可扩展、灵活的解决方案，并具有移动性的独特优势”。这一特性使其特别适用于以下场景：1）电网基础设施薄弱或扩建受阻的沿海地区（如美国和欧洲部分地区），可大幅缩短从规划到投产的时间；2）需要临时性大算力部署的事件（如大型赛事、科研项目）；3）作为弹性补充，与陆地数据中心形成灾备或峰值负载分流。此外，利用海水自然冷却可显著降低数据中心能耗（PUE），符合全球ESG趋势。

不过，该技术仍处于早期验证阶段。除了要解决盐雾腐蚀、长期振动对硬件的损耗等工程问题外，其商业可行性还取决于LNG燃料成本、海上运维体系以及监管政策的完善程度。三星重工正借助劳氏认证体系推动行业标准建立，而超微电脑的服务器可靠性验证结果将直接影响运营商和投资人的信心。随着全球AI算力需求爆发式增长和陆地资源约束加剧，浮动数据中心有望成为补充陆地数据中心的关键技术路径之一。

history.title: Analyzing TSMC's fab expansion roadmap — multi-fab N2 ramp, CoWoS, SoIC, and uncorking bottlenecks
history.description: TSMC is executing the largest manufacturing expansion in semiconductor industry history that combines simultaneous multi-fab N2 ramps, AI-driven manufacturing optimizations, and massive CoWoS/SoIC packaging capacity expansion to meet increasing demand for AI accelerators.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter When we referred to TSMC just several years ago, we called it 'the world's largest foundry,' implying that Intel was still the world's largest producer of advanced logic chips. However, having spent nearly $240 billion on capacity expansion over the last 10 years, TSMC now has nine sites with dozens of 300-mm fabs, many of which can process orders of magnitude more wafers using EUV-based process technologies than Intel*, which makes TSMC the world's largest maker of advanced logic chips.Being the world's largest maker of advanced AI processors requires TSMC to stay ahead of its rivals, Intel and Samsung Foundry, both in terms of process technologies and, perhaps, even more importantly, in terms of production capacity.Therefore, TSMC has kicked off the most aggressive manufacturing expansion in its history as the company races to meet explosive demand for AI processors, logic chips made on leading-edge nodes, and advanced packaging.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: TSMC)During TSMC's Tech Symposium 2026 manufacturing presentations, the company revealed that in 2025 – 2026, it effectively doubled its historical construction pace, building or converting nine fab phases annually, up from an average of four phases per year. The company is simultaneously building or ramping new fabs in Taiwan, the U.S., Japan, and Germany. In addition, it introduces new ways to improve the productivity of existing facilities.Swipe to scroll horizontallyTSMC's new or ramping production facilitiesSite NamePhaseCapabilitiesFab LocationStatus Fab 201, 2A16, N2Hsinchu, TaiwanRamping Fab 212N3Phoenix, ArizonaEquipping Fab 213, 4A16, N2Phoenix, ArizonaIn constructionFab 22 1A16, N2Kaohsiung, TaiwanRampingFab 222, 3A16, N2Kaohsiung, TaiwanEquipped, ramping in H2 2026Fab 23 - JASM2Down to N3Kumamoto, JapanIn construction as of January 2025. Construction stalled.Fab 24 - ESMC1N12, N16, N22, N28Dresden, GermanyIn construction as of August 2024 Fab 251A14, A13, A12Taichung, TaiwanIn constructionN2 ramp: Six-figure amounts of wafers per-month by 2029The central part of TSMC's expansion plan is its N2 process technology. At present, the company is ramping up production of chips using N2 at two sites: Fab 20 phase 1 and phase 2 in Hsinchu near TSMC's global R&D center, and Fab 22 phase 1 in Kaohsiung. Ramping a leading-edge node at three facilities simultaneously is highly uncommon for foundries. The company also plans to ramp up production at Fab 22 phase 2 shortly and Fab 22 phase 3 by the end of the year. Eventually, Fab 22 phase 4 will come online as well. As a result, TSMC aims to start mass production on its N2 process technology at five facilities in the first year, which is at an unprecedented scale. You may like TSMC ups revenue guidance and CapEx, buoyed by 'multiyear AI megatrend' Leading-edge foundry roadmaps for TSMC, Intel and Samsung — outlining the path to 1.4nm nodes and beyond Intel's EMIB-T packaging technology set for fab rollout this year Image 1 of 4(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)As a result of such an aggressive ramp, TSMC expects its N2 wafer-out capacity to be 45% higher than that of N3B in the first year. Reports from 2023 – 2024 indicate that TSMC ramped its N3B production at two or three phases of Fab 18 in 2023 and reached a capacity of around 60,000 wafer starts per month by the end of that year. If the reports are accurate, then TSMC expects its N2 capacity to reach around 90,000 wafer starts per month (WSPM) by the end of the year. This exceeds the fully ramped capacity of Intel's 18A-capable Fab 52, which is believed to be at around 40,000 WSPM.What is even more impressive is that TSMC intends to increase its N2/A16-capable capacity by 70% every year through 2028, which means hundreds of thousands of WSPM in 2029.In addition to reaching vast capacity, ramping up five fab phases simultaneously enables TSMC to mitigate risks. If one fab phase experiences a contamination issue, tool failure, or yield issues, the entire N2 supply chain will not collapse. The same applies to ramping up production at two sites located in different parts of the country: an earthquake or utility failure can interrupt production or even cause yield loss at one of them, but it will not affect another. Such risk mitigation is critically important when customers like Apple, AMD, Nvidia, or Qualcomm, which demand a continuous supply. There is potentially another bonus with ramping up these fab phases in parallel rather than in serial, so read on.N2 ramp: One Team and the Super Manufacturing PlatformSuch an unusual ramp strategy seems to be enabled by two programs at TSMC: the "One Team" collaboration between R&D and fab operations, and the Super Manufacturing Platform (SMP) that enables multiple fabs (or rather fab phases) to work as one, which likely has similarities to Intel's "Copy Exactly." TSMC hasn't shared many details about the One Team and SMP, though we can make some educated guesses.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)The One Team is a global manufacturing knowledge-transfer system that links R&D, process integration, equipment management, and high-volume manufacturing expertise during technology development and ramp-up. To speed up feedback loops, TSMC likely inserts manufacturing teams relatively early in node development so that R&D teams adjust their work to what is possible at fabs. As a result, yield learning, process optimization, and tool productivity improvements can be done quickly at one fab and then transferred to others. TSMC says that One Team enabled a 20% faster technology transfer compared to N3, without disclosing the time it typically takes to transfer technology from one fab to another. What to read next TSMC's details next-gen CoWoS roadmap: over 14-reticle packages and 48x leap in compute power expected by 2029 Why TSMC grew four times faster than its foundry rivals in 2025 TSMC SoIC 3D stacking roadmap outlines path from 6-micron pitches today to 4.5-micron in 2029 In addition, all of TSMC's GigaFab sites now rely on its Super Manufacturing Platform (SMP), which is essentially a centralized manufacturing-control system that makes multiple fabs operate as one giant synchronized fab with standard process recipes, tool configurations, metrology, and yield management flows. This should enable TSMC to transfer production between fabs more easily, ramp new nodes faster, introduce yield fixes globally instead of locally, and reduce customer requalification work when production of chips is moved from one fab to another.Moreover, since every fab phase generates its own tool behavior data, defect density data, process window statistics, and yield learning information, multiple simultaneous ramps may actually accelerate yield/defects learning when SMP and One Team are in place. In turn, it may speed up ramping of fab phases.A 70% CAGR in N2/A16 capacity in the coming years is an extraordinarily aggressive ramp for leading-edge manufacturing. Without something like TSMC's' One Team structure and SMP, coordinating that scale of expansion across multiple Fab 22 phases, Fab 20, and eventually Fab 21 phase 3 in Arizona would be barely possible both from organizational (operational control) and from economic (yield learning, process window, etc.) points of view.TSMC also noted that despite the significantly higher complexity associated with gate-all-around (GAA) nanosheet transistors, N2 is achieving a better yield learning curve than N3, which again can be attributed to the innovative approaches that the company uses.Beyond N2: A14, A13, and A12TSMC's N2/N2P/N2X/N2U/A16 production will largely be concentrated at Fab 20 phase 1 and 2, Fab 22 phases 1, 2, 3, 4, and, to some degree, Fab 21 phase 3. However, for nodes beyond 2nm-class (A16 is essentially N2P with a backside power delivery network), such as A14, A13, and A12, TSMC will build Fab 21 phase 3 and then the all-new Fab 25 site in central Taiwan with at least four phases. (Image credit: TSMC)A14 is set to start high-volume production in late 2028, so there is a good chance that TSMC will ramp it at both Fab 20 phase 3 and Fab 25 phase 1. However, given the company's aggressive approach to capacity expansion, TSMC might well surprise us once again. Also, we do not yet know how TSMC plans to upgrade N2/A16-capable fabs to subsequent nodes, if at all.Expansion beyond N2The expansion is not limited to the N2 production node and subsequent technologies. TSMC is continuing to grow combined N3 and N5 capacity at a 25% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) from 2022 through 2027. To address immediate demand, the company is converting some N5 capacity into N3 production, which is not particularly expensive, since N3 reuses 85% - 90% of the tools used for N5. Furthermore, as much of TSMC's N3 and N5 capacities are concentrated at Fab 18 (four phases N5, four phases N3), converting some of the N5 capacity to N3 is relatively easy from a logistical perspective.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)AI is here to help (to build more AI processors)Alongside the conversion of N5 to N3 capacity, TSMC also heavily uses AI to improve the performance of each tool, and the whole fab in particular. Essentially, TSMC uses AI to build more AI processors, which seems to be a paradox, but it is becoming popularized as AI becomes embedded within workflows.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)One of the things that greatly slows down cycle times of modern fabs is batch processing of wafers in various chambers, something that is an inevitable part of some 5,000 steps. Essentially, 25 wafers ‘wait’ in a (perhaps in a CVD chamber) for a lithography tool to process them individually.Atsuyoshi Koike of Rapidus thinks differently and believes that a single-wafer processing across all steps can significantly speed up cycle time, but at the cost of tool efficiency. TSMC does not seem to plan to use single-wafer processing (despite its purchasing power, it can likely persuade fab tool makers to produce appropriate tools), but it can certainly optimize the ways in which it uses existing tools to boost the productivity of its existing fabs.TSMC revealed at its recent Technology Summit that it uses intelligent scheduling systems that incorporate 'state-of-the-art linear programming and heuristic algorithms' to optimize equipment efficiency, though it did not reveal what exactly is done and what is achieved. TSMC further revealed that it uses generative AI algorithms to identify optimal parameters that 'challenge the physical limits of equipment' while maintaining wafer quality. In parallel, the company analyzes tool logs using big-data analytics and text-mining systems to dynamically adjust key parameters, minimize tool idle time, and maximize output.AI systems are also used for real-time chamber condition analysis to determine optimal chamber-cleaning timing and avoid unnecessary maintenance that could reduce machine uptime and available capacity. In addition, TSMC disclosed that AI-assisted comparison and fine-tuning of large volumes of machine verification parameters reduced the time required to validate new tools and reach high-volume manufacturing by more than 20%, which helps to ramp up new fab modules faster.TSMC also said it achieved more flexible allocation and higher combined N3 and N5 capacity at Fab 18 in Tainan by increasing equipment commonality and integrating 'cross-technologies planning,' which essentially means that the company re-uses as many tools as possible.Expansion beyond TaiwanOutside of Taiwan, TSMC continues to broaden its geographic footprint. In Arizona, Fab 21 phase 1 is already producing chips using N4 technology (with capacity increasing by 1.8X this year alone), while Fab 21 phase 2 is on track to start N3 production in Q3 2027. Fab 21 phase 3 targets N2 sometime later this decade, as the company continues to construct shells both for phase 3 and phase 4. The company also reaffirmed plans for an advanced packaging facility, an R&D center, and additional land acquisitions to support future expansion.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)In Japan, the company’s Kumamoto Fab 23 phase 1 is already producing 28nm and 22nm chips, while Fab 23 phase 2 underwent a major strategic shift. Originally planned for 7nm-class production, the facility will instead manufacture using technologies down to N3 3nm to address stronger-than-expected local demand and onshoring intentions of local chip designers.Meanwhile, Fab 23 in Dresden, Germany, which is under construction, is aimed at automotive and industrial applications with legacy planar transistors and FinFET-based 28nm, 22nm, N16, and N12 production nodes.Advanced PackagingAI itself is now one of the main drivers behind the company's unprecedented capacity growth. TSMC disclosed that wafer shipments for AI accelerators are expected to rise 11X between 2022 and 2026. The company also highlighted the rapid growth of extremely large dies exceeding 500 mm2, as shipments of those devices are projected to increase 6X over the same period. Such products typically require lots of wafer capacity (wafer starts) and advanced packaging technologies, since many of these designs use HBM3E memory.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)(Image credit: TSMC)Advanced packaging has therefore become just as important as wafer fabrication itself. TSMC said its CoWoS capacity will grow at an 80% CAGR between 2022 and 2027, while SoIC capacity will expand at a 90% CAGR during the same timeframe. TSMC also said it has improved development-to-HVM transition times by 30% for CoWoS and by 75% for SoIC compared to earlier generations.TSMC currently operates 11 advanced packaging facilities in Taiwan (AP1 in Hsinchu, AP2A/AP2B/AP2C and AP8 in Tainan, AP3 in Longtan, AP5 in Taichung, AP6A/AP6B/AP6C in Zhunan, and AP7 in Chiayi). According to a recent DigiTimes report, the company is simultaneously expanding multiple advanced packaging campuses, including AP5, AP6, AP7, and AP8.The AP7 site in Chiayi will reportedly become TSMC's largest advanced packaging campus using SoIC to support major customers like Nvidia, which plans to use 3D packaging technologies for its next-generation Feynman GPUs. AP8 — converted from a former Innolux LCD fab — is expected to exceed 40,000 wafers per month of CoWoS capacity by late 2026.While CoWoS is the de facto standard for AI processors, SoIC is set to become much more widely used in the coming years. As a result, the company is also rapidly expanding its SoIC production capacity. DigiTimes claims that AP6 in Zhunan could approach 10,000 SoIC wafers per month, whereas AP7B may add approximately 12,000 wafers per month. Future AP7 phases are expected to support both SoIC and CoPoS technologies, though CoPoS is a part of TSMC's roadmap in the 2030s.Advanced packaging now requires tight ecosystem integration that includes HBM suppliers, substrate vendors, OSAT partners, testing companies, materials providers, and toolmakers, with which TSMC works to standardize those tools. The very emergence of such an ecosystem emphasizes the increasing role of TSMC in the burgeoning AI industry.An all-encompassing roadmapAfter investing nearly $240 billion into capacity expansion over the last decade, TSMC has evolved from the world’s largest foundry into the world's largest producer of advanced logic chips, producing the lion's share of AI processors today. (Image credit: TSMC)To support the explosive AI demand and to stay ahead of Intel and Samsung Electronics, TSMC has doubled its historical fab construction pace to nine fab phases annually in 2025 – 2026 while simultaneously expanding in Taiwan, Arizona, Japan, and Germany. The company's N2 ramp is unprecedented as the company preps to ramp five fab phases within the node's first year, and N2/A16 capacity is projected to grow at a 70% CAGR through 2028.TSMC said this aggressive expansion is enabled by its One Team organizational structure and Super Manufacturing Platform (SMP), which synchronizes manufacturing, yield learning, and process control across multiple fabs. The company is also implementing various AI-driven manufacturing optimizations, including intelligent scheduling systems, generative AI process tuning, and real-time tool analytics to improve throughput, reduce cycle times, and accelerate tool qualification.At the same time, TSMC is rapidly expanding advanced packaging capacities. The company intends to increase CoWoS and SoIC capacities at 80% and 90% CAGR, respectively, through 2027, as demand for both technologies is expected to grow as chiplet-based designs and HBM memory are technologies of choice for AI accelerators.*TSMC's wafer processing revenue for 2025 was $103,708.5 billion, thus representing around 84% of consolidated revenue of $122.4 billion. EUV-based N3 and N5 process technologies accounted for 60% of TSMC's wafer revenue in 2025, thus earning around $62,225 billion. Intel Foundry earned $17.826 billion in 2025, $307 million came from external customers that mainly ordered advanced packaging. It is estimated that process technologies that use EUV account for more than 10% but less than 20% of Intel's wafer revenue.Intel does not disclose a revenue split similar to TSMC's (wafer fabrication vs. packaging/testing vs. other) in its official filings, so its wafer processing revenue is hard to estimate, especially given the fact that some of its silicon is made at TSMC and is packaged internally. Nonetheless, even 20% of Intel Foundry's 2025 revenue is $3.565 billion, which is over 17 times less than TSMC earns on its EUV-based nodes.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 分析台积电晶圆厂扩张路线图——多晶圆厂N2量产爬坡、CoWoS、SoIC及解除瓶颈
history.descriptionZh:
在过去的十年间，台积电（TSMC）投入了近2400亿美元用于产能扩张，如今已拥有九个生产基地、数十座300毫米晶圆厂，其中许多工厂在基于极紫外光刻（EUV）的工艺节点上的晶圆处理能力远超英特尔（Intel）。这使得台积电不仅成为全球最大的晶圆代工厂，更跃升为全球最大的先进逻辑芯片制造商，尤其在AI处理器生产领域占据主导地位。为了维持对竞争对手英特尔和三星代工（Samsung Foundry）的领先优势，并且应对AI处理器、先进节点逻辑芯片及先进封装需求的爆发式增长，台积电已启动其史上最激进的制造扩张计划。

在台积电2026年技术研讨会（Tech Symposium）上，公司透露在2025至2026年间，其历史性建设速度翻倍：每年建造或转换九个晶圆厂模块，而以往平均每年仅四个。台积电同步在中国台湾、美国亚利桑那州、日本熊本和德国德累斯顿建设或投产新工厂。同时，公司还引入新方法提高现有设施的产能。

**N2工艺：首年五厂并行，产能远超以往**

台积电扩张计划的核心是其N2工艺。目前，公司正在两个地点加速N2芯片的产量爬坡：新竹的Fab 20第一期和第二期（靠近全球研发中心），以及高雄的Fab 22第一期。同时为三个设施同时启动先进节点的产量爬坡，这在代工业极为罕见。台积电还计划很快在高雄启动Fab 22第二期，并在年底前启动第三期，随后第四期也将上线。这意味着在N2工艺量产的第一年，台积电将在五个晶圆厂模块同时大规模生产，规模史无前例。

由于这种激进的爬坡节奏，台积电预计其N2首年晶圆产出量将比N3B首年高出45%。根据2023-2024年的报道，N3B在2023年于Fab 18的两到三个模块爬坡，年底产能约为每月6万片晶圆（WSPM）。如果报道准确，那么台积电预计今年底N2产能将达每月约9万片，超过英特尔18A工艺Fab 52（约为4万WSPM）的全产能。更令人印象深刻的是，台积电计划在2028年前，使N2/A16容量每年增长70%，这意味着到2029年月产能可达数十万片。

同时并行五个模块的爬坡还能分散风险：若某模块出现污染、设备故障或良率问题，整个N2供应链不会崩溃；不同地点的工厂分布还能应对地震或公用事业中断等风险。这对苹果、AMD、英伟达、高通等要求持续供应的客户至关重要。

**“一个团队”与“超级制造平台”：技术转移提速20%**

如此不寻常的爬坡策略得益于台积电的两项计划：“一个团队”（One Team）——研发与晶圆厂运营的协作机制，以及“超级制造平台”（Super Manufacturing Platform，SMP）——使多个工厂（或模块）如同一体化工厂运行，类似于英特尔的“精确复制”（Copy Exactly）策略。

“一个团队”是一个全球制造知识传递系统，在技术开发与爬坡期间将研发、工艺整合、设备管理和大规模制造专长联系起来。为了加快反馈循环，台积电让制造团队在节点开发早期介入，使研发工作尽可能贴近工厂实际。这使良率学习、工艺优化和设备生产力提升能在单一工厂快速完成，然后转移到其他工厂。台积电表示，相比N3，“一个团队”使技术转移速度提升了20%。

所有台积电GigaFab站点现均依赖SMP——一个集中的制造控制系统，使多个工厂以标准化工艺配方、工具配置、计量和良率管理流程，像一台同步化巨型工厂那样运行。这使产能转移更简便，新节点爬坡更快，良率修复可全球而非局部实施，且减少了芯片生产在不同工厂间转移时客户的重新认证工作。此外，由于每个工厂模块会产生独特的工具行为数据、缺陷密度数据、工艺窗口统计和良率学习信息，在SMP和“一个团队”的协同下，多个同时爬坡反而可能加速良率/缺陷学习，进而加快工厂模块的爬坡速度。

如果没有“一个团队”和SMP的架构，要在多个Fab 22模块、Fab 20以及未来的Fab 21第三期之间协调N2/A16年复合增长率70%的扩张，无论从组织运营控制还是从经济角度（良率学习、工艺窗口等）都几乎不可能。台积电还指出，尽管环绕栅极（GAA）纳米片晶体管的复杂性显著增加，N2的良率学习曲线甚至优于N3，这同样归功于公司采用的创新方法。

**N2之后：A14、A13、A12及未来**

N2/N2P/N2X/N2U/A16的生产将主要集中在新竹Fab 20第一、二期，高雄Fab 22第一至四期，以及一定程度上亚利桑那Fab 21第三期。对于2纳米级之后的节点（A16本质上是N2P加上背面供电网络），如A14、A13、A12，台积电将在Fab 21第三期后建设全新的Fab 25（位于台湾中部，至少四个模块）。A14计划于2028年底开始大规模生产，很可能在Fab 20第三期和Fab 25第一期同时爬坡。考虑到公司激进的产能策略，未来可能再次带来惊喜。

**N3/N5持续增长，AI赋能制造**

N2之外，台积电的N3和N5联合产能2022至2027年年复合增长率达25%。为满足即时需求，公司正将部分N5产能转换为N3，因为N3可重复使用85%至90%的N5工具，且两者在Fab 18集中，转换物流上相对容易。

台积电还大量使用人工智能（AI）提升每个工具乃至整个工厂的性能——即“用AI来制造更多AI处理器”。这看似矛盾，但正随着AI嵌入工作流程而普及。现代晶圆厂中，批处理流程（如25片晶圆在CVD腔室等待光刻工具逐一处理）会大幅拖慢周期时间。台积电并未计划采用单片晶圆处理（那会牺牲工具效率），而是通过智能调度系统（采用最先进的线性规划和启发式算法）优化设备效率。公司还使用生成式AI算法，在确保晶圆质量的前提下找到挑战设备物理极限的最优参数。同时，利用大数据分析和文本挖掘系统分析工具日志，动态调整关键参数，最小化工具空闲时间，最大化产出。AI系统还用于实时腔室状态分析，以确定最佳清洁时机，避免不必要的维护提高机器可用时间。此外，AI辅助的大规模机器验证参数比较与微调，使新工具从验证到大规模生产的验证时间缩短了20%以上，加速了新建工厂模块的爬坡。通过增加设备通用性和“跨技术规划”，台积电还实现了台南Fab 18的N3和N5联合容量的更灵活分配与更高总量。

**全球布局扩展**

在中国台湾以外，亚利桑那Fab 21第一期已使用N4技术生产芯片（今年容量将增长1.8倍），第二期计划2027年第三季度启动N3生产，第三期瞄准本十年晚些时候的N2。公司还确认了先进封装设施、研发中心及额外土地收购的计划。在日本，熊本Fab 23第一期已生产28nm/22nm芯片，第二期战略转向：原计划7nm级，现调整为到N3以应对当地强劲的本地化需求。德国德累斯顿的Fab 23正在建设，针对汽车和工业应用，采用28nm、22nm、N16、N12等成熟平面晶体管和FinFET节点。

**先进封装：CoWoS和SoIC以80%-90%年复合增长率扩张**

AI本身是台积电产能增长的主要驱动力之一。公司披露，AI加速器的晶圆出货量预计2022至2026年增长11倍。超过500mm²的超大型芯片出货量同期增长6倍，这些产品通常需要大量晶圆产能和先进封装（如HBM3E内存）。先进封装的重要性不亚于晶圆制造本身。台积电表示，其CoWoS产能2022至2027年年复合增长率达80%，SoIC产能年复合增长率达90%。与上一代相比，CoWoS从开发到大规模生产的过渡时间改善了30%，SoIC改善了75%。

台积电目前在中国台湾运营11座先进封装设施。据DigiTimes报道，公司同时扩建多个封装园区：嘉义AP7将成为最大的SoIC封装园区，支持英伟达下一代Feynman GPU（采用3D封装技术）；AP8（由原群创LCD工厂改造）预计2026年底CoWoS月产能超4万片。SoIC产能方面，竹南AP6可能接近每月1万片，AP7B可能增加约1.2万片。未来AP7模块将同时支持SoIC和CoPoS技术（后者属于2030年代路线图）。先进封装现在需要紧密的生态系统整合，包括HBM供应商、基板厂商、OSAT合作伙伴、测试公司、材料供应商和工具制造商，台积电与这些伙伴合作推动工具标准化。

**总结：从最大代工厂到AI芯片制造核心**

在投入近2400亿美元后，台积电已从世界最大代工厂转变为全球最大的先进逻辑芯片制造商，生产着当今AI处理器的绝大部分份额。为支撑AI需求的爆炸式增长，并领先英特尔和三星电子，台积电在2025-2026年将历史建设速度翻倍至每年九个晶圆厂模块，同时在中国台湾、亚利桑那、日本和德国扩张。N2爬坡史无前例：第一年准备启动五个模块，N2/A16容量预计2028年前年复合增长率达70%。公司表示，这得益于“一个团队”组织结构和“超级制造平台”（同步制造、良率学习和流程控制）的支撑。同时，台积电还部署了智能调度、生成式AI工艺调谐、实时工具分析等多种AI驱动制造优化方案，以提升产能、缩短周期、加速工具认证。在先进封装方面，CoWoS和SoIC容量分别以80%和90%的年复合增长率扩张，以满足基于小芯片设计和HBM内存的AI加速器需求。作为参考，台积电2025年晶圆加工收入约1037.085亿美元，占合并收入1224亿美元的84%，其中基于EUV的N3和N5工艺贡献晶圆收入的60%（约622.25亿美元）。相比之下，英特尔代工2025年收入178.26亿美元，外部客户收入仅3.07亿美元（主要来自先进封装），其EUV工艺收入估计远低于台积电。台积电正以无与伦比的规模和速度重塑全球先进芯片制造格局。

history.title: China drafts $295 billion plan to build national AI data center grid running on 80% homemade silicon — projected 2028 timeline could run into limits of local chip production
history.description: China is drafting a plan to spend roughly 2 trillion yuan over five years on a nationwide grid of AI data centers.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter China is drafting a plan to spend roughly 2 trillion yuan ($295 billion) over five years on a nationwide web of AI data centers. The goal is for at least 80% of the underlying technology, AI chips included, to be sourced from domestic suppliers such as Huawei, according to a Bloomberg report citing people familiar with the discussions.The National Development and Reform Commission is responsible for the blueprint of this network, while state carriers China Mobile and China Telecom will operate most of the facilities and link them up to a single computing grid by 2028. The build-out of this grid leans heavily on sovereign debt and ultra-long special government bonds. Folding in power grid upgrades could push the total capital requirement beyond 5 trillion yuan, those sources told Bloomberg. Funding the build-out is easy, though; filling them with domestic accelerators is a different story. The 80% domestic sourcing requirement effectively locks out Nvidia and AMD accelerators, so China will be capped by whatever amount of chips SMIC can physically produce. The foundry’s most advanced stable node remains its N+2 process, which is roughly equivalent to 7nm and is currently running above 93% utilization, leaving little headroom as every government-certified Chinese chipmaker competes for the same wafer slots.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Another major chokepoint is high-bandwidth memory. Highly limited domestic HBM supply constrains how many Ascend-class accelerators Huawei can assemble. Huawei alone shipped around 812,000 chips last year and projects some $12 billion in processor revenue for 2026, a pace that its own supply chain has struggled to sustain. It’s estimated that China’s domestic suppliers will cover only around 76% of all Chinese AI chip demand by 2030, even as that market grows toward $67 billion.Beijing has massively tightened its restrictions on foreign silicon in a series of new controls. Last August, Beijing introduced a requirement that data centers source at least 50% of chips locally, and by November, state-funded projects were barred from foreign accelerators entirely, with builds less than 30% complete reportedly told to strip out Nvidia, AMD, and Intel parts. You may like Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo Half of planned US data center builds have been delayed or canceled, growth limited by shortages of power infrastructure and parts from China Chinese chip industry leaders admit the country lags five to ten years behind in AI data center chips China's own industry has questioned whether domestic hardware can keep pace. SMIC co-CEO Zhao Haijun has cautioned that the rush to add capacity risks leaving data centers idle, comparing it to building highways ahead of the traffic. Chinese chip executives have separately conceded the country trails the leading edge in AI data center silicon by five to 10 years. When DeepSeek was steered toward Huawei hardware for model training, it eventually reverted to Nvidia hardware, lending credence to the idea that domestic parts still struggle with the heaviest training workloads, even where they suffice for inference.Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 中国起草2950亿美元计划，建设80%使用国产芯片的人工智能数据中心网络——预计2028年时间线可能遭遇本地芯片生产限制
history.descriptionZh:
中国正计划未来五年投入约2万亿人民币（约合2950亿美元），建设覆盖全国的AI数据中心网络。根据彭博社援引知情人士消息，该计划要求至少80%的底层技术（包括AI芯片）来自华为等国内供应商。国家发改委负责该网络的蓝图设计，而中国移动和中国电信将运营大部分设施，并计划在2028年前将它们连接成一个统一的计算网格。这一建设主要依赖主权债务和超长期特别国债，若将电网升级成本计算在内，总资金需求可能超过5万亿人民币。

然而，尽管资金筹措相对容易，但为这些数据中心配备国产加速器是另一大挑战。80%的国产化要求实质上排除了英伟达和AMD的加速器，因此中国的AI芯片产能将受限于中芯国际（SMIC）的实际生产能力。目前，中芯国际最先进的稳定工艺节点是N+2，大致相当于7纳米工艺，其产线利用率已超过93%，几乎没有闲置产能。所有经过政府认证的中国芯片制造商都在争夺同样的晶圆产能，这使得产能瓶颈极为突出。

另一个关键瓶颈是高带宽内存（HBM）。国内HBM供应严重受限，这直接制约了华为能够组装的天罡（Ascend）系列加速器数量。华为去年单独出货了约81.2万颗芯片，并预计到2026年处理器收入将达到120亿美元，但这一速度其自身供应链已难以维持。据估计，即使到2030年，国内供应商也只能覆盖中国AI芯片总需求的约76%，而该市场届时将增长至670亿美元。

北京方面已通过一系列新管制措施大幅收紧对外国芯片的限制。去年8月，中国要求数据中心至少50%的芯片必须来自国内；到11月，国有资助项目被完全禁止使用外国加速器，且据报道，建设进度不足30%的项目被要求拆除英伟达、AMD和英特尔的部件。中国本土产业界也对国产硬件能否跟上需求提出了质疑。中芯国际联席CEO赵海军曾警告，急于扩充产能可能导致数据中心闲置，他将其比喻为在车流到来之前先修好高速公路。多家中国芯片企业高管也承认，中国在AI数据中心芯片领域落后领先水平5到10年。一个典型案例是，DeepSeek在尝试使用华为硬件进行模型训练后，最终又回到了英伟达硬件上，这印证了国产芯片在处理最繁重的训练任务时仍面临困难，尽管在推理任务上可能已经足够。

总体而言，这一庞大的国家计划体现了中国在AI基础设施上实现自主可控的坚定决心，但受限于国内先进制程产能、HBM供应以及芯片设计能力，实际执行中面临巨大挑战。该计划将推动国产AI芯片生态加速发展，但短期内仍难以完全替代外国产品，尤其在高端训练场景下。与此同时，电网升级等配套投资将进一步拉动内需，并可能重塑中国数字基础设施的格局。

history.title: NVIDIA Confidential Computing to Help Expand Apple’s Private Cloud Compute
history.description: NVIDIA GPUs with Confidential Computing are now used for confidential inference in Apple’s Private Cloud Compute (PCC), as it expands beyond Apple’s data centers to Google Cloud.  Unveiled during Apple’s annual WWDC gathering for developers from around the globe, NVIDIA GPUs will support server-side inference for Apple Foundation Models, custom-built by Apple and Google, leveraging […]
history.content: NVIDIA GPUs with Confidential Computing are now used for confidential inference in Apple’s Private Cloud Compute (PCC), as it expands beyond Apple’s data centers to Google Cloud. Unveiled during Apple’s annual WWDC gathering for developers from around the globe, NVIDIA GPUs will support server-side inference for Apple Foundation Models, custom-built by Apple and Google, leveraging the technologies behind the Gemini family of models. NVIDIA is collaborating with Apple and Google to support some of the next-generation Apple Intelligence features, using NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs with Confidential Computing integrated into Private Cloud Compute’s hardware security architecture running on Google Cloud. Confidential Computing Matters for the Era of AI Experiences NVIDIA Confidential Computing provides a hardware-based security layer for accelerated AI workloads. The technology protects data while it’s being processed by isolating workloads in trusted execution environments and enabling systems to cryptographically verify that the infrastructure has not been tampered with before any sensitive data is sent to the server. For end users, NVIDIA Confidential Computing means that no one, not even the system’s builders, can look at their data, chats or conversations. Adoption of NVIDIA Confidential Computing at this scale reflects a broader shift in AI infrastructure: As AI experiences combine on-device and cloud-based processing for their tasks, there’s a need for high-performance, server-side inference while maintaining strong privacy and security guarantees. How Confidential Computing Enforces Privacy and Trust NVIDIA Confidential Computing reflects NVIDIA’s commitment to trustworthy AI and includes these key capabilities: Hardware-rooted trust, helping establish that systems are running on genuine, untampered NVIDIA GPUs. Encrypted communication paths, helping protect data as it moves between components. Remote attestation, enabling software to verify the security state of the platform before releasing sensitive data. Support for accelerated AI inference and training, helping organizations run privacy-sensitive workloads without moving away from GPU performance. These capabilities are increasingly relevant for AI services that need to process sensitive information while maintaining strong user privacy controls. Learn more about NVIDIA Confidential Computing and NVIDIA AI cybersecurity solutions.

history.titleZh: NVIDIA 机密计算助力扩展苹果私有云计算
history.descriptionZh:
NVIDIA现已将搭载机密计算（Confidential Computing）功能的GPU部署于苹果的私有云计算（Private Cloud Compute, PCC）平台，用于执行机密推理任务，且该平台已从苹果自有的数据中心扩展至Google Cloud。这一消息在苹果全球开发者大会（WWDC）期间公布：NVIDIA GPU将支持苹果基础模型（Apple Foundation Models）的服务器端推理——这些模型由苹果与Google基于Gemini系列模型技术联合定制。具体而言，NVIDIA与苹果、Google合作，使用NVIDIA Blackwell GPU（集成机密计算能力）融入PCC的硬件安全架构，并运行在Google Cloud基础设施之上，以支撑下一代Apple Intelligence的若干功能。

**机密计算为何在AI体验时代至关重要**  
NVIDIA机密计算为加速AI工作负载提供了一层硬件级安全保障。其核心技术在于：通过将工作负载隔离在可信执行环境（Trusted Execution Environment）中，保护数据在处理过程中的安全；同时，系统能够在发送任何敏感数据至服务器前，通过密码学方式验证基础设施是否被篡改。对最终用户而言，这意味着即便系统构建者也无法查看其数据、聊天记录或对话内容。NVIDIA机密计算在此规模下的采用，反映出AI基础设施的深刻变革——当AI体验需要融合设备端与云端处理时，既需要高性能的服务器端推理，又必须坚守强大的隐私与安全承诺。

**机密计算如何实现隐私与信任**  
NVIDIA机密计算体现了其对可信AI的承诺，包含以下关键能力：  
- **硬件根信任**：帮助确认系统运行在真实、未被篡改的NVIDIA GPU上。  
- **加密通信路径**：保护数据在组件之间传输时的安全。  
- **远程证明**：使软件在释放敏感数据前能够验证平台的安全状态。  
- **加速AI推理与训练支持**：帮助组织在不牺牲GPU性能的前提下运行隐私敏感型工作负载。

这些能力对于需要处理敏感信息、同时保持严格用户隐私控制的AI服务而言愈发关键。欲了解更多，可查阅NVIDIA机密计算及NVIDIA AI网络安全解决方案的详细资料。

history.content: At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data centers. The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It’s releasing a new commercial energy storage system strategy, anchored by newly developed sodium-ion batteries for industrial-scale grid applications. And it’s launching a new feature for EV owners that it says will help simplify public charging.Right now, millions of EVs are sitting idly in driveways across the country with a wealth of electrons stored in their batteries. GM is betting that even as EV sales cool down, public utilities will want to work with automakers to utilize those EV batteries as a potential solution to the energy demand crisis they face. It was also the latest effort by the largest automaker in North America to grab a piece of the multibillion-dollar energy generation and storage market, which it has been trying to do for nearly four years now.“We see a future where electric vehicles, batteries that power them, and the country’s power grids work together,” GM’s chief product officer Sterling Anderson said in prepared remarks for today’s event.EVs are unique in their ability to send energy back to the grid, just as they pull it while charging. Many EVs are built with this bidirectional charging capability, enabling the two-way flow of energy. In essence, it treats high-capacity lithium-ion batteries not only as tools to power EVs but also as backup storage cells to charge other electric devices, an entire home, or even to send power to the electrical grid for possible energy savings.As AI data centers put more stress on the grid, GM thinks its hundreds of electric vehicles can help lighten the load. The automaker says that with bidirectional charging capabilities, EVs can send energy back into the grid during times of peak demand. As such, the automaker says it will release a firmware update to give its current vehicle-to-home system customers the ability to send energy back to the grid (vehicle-to-grid, or V2G). GM customers who already own the equipment will receive the update automatically.GM says there are currently over 250,000 bidirectional-capable Chevy, Cadillac, and GMC EVs on American roads today. Theoretically, their combined battery capacity is enough to power 120,000 homes for up to an entire week.GM is already testing this theory in two states. In Northern California, the company is partnering with PG&E to develop a localized fleet of 52,000 EVs for “grid balancing protocols,” which it says will be operational by 2030. And in Michigan, GM is working with DTE Energy to “stress-test” bidirectional charging using 30 of its own employees’ homes as real-world test cases. In addition to providing a benefit to public utilities, the automaker says EV owners could see a financial windfall too.“By injecting flexibility into a historically rigid system, V2G technology simultaneously can lower aggregate energy costs, create a potential financial return for the consumer, and enhance the systemic reliability of the broader grid,” Anderson said.But enabling V2G technology isn’t as easy as flipping a switch. In an open letter, GM Energy VP Wade Sheffer urged regulators to formalize V2G infrastructure, citing International Energy Agency (IEA) reports identifying V2G as the technology with the largest hourly flexibility to limit future grid investment costs. Sheffer said that the auto industry needs to work with government to educate the public to the benefits of V2G tech. And utilities must simplify the administrative process to allow their customers to seamlessly enroll in future projects.GM says it’s also working on new industrial-scale solutions, partnering with California-based Peak Energy to develop and deploy sodium ion chemistry for energy storage systems. Sodium is seen by some as an improvement over lithium, both in terms of availability and stability. The material is more cost-effective to obtain and isn’t subject to the same safety hazards as lithium, which can catch fire under certain circumstances. They also perform better in cold weather than Li-ion batteries. Some major battery makers, like China’s CATL, believe that sodium-ion batteries could potentially replace up to half the market for lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries that now dominates the field.PreviousNext1/6GM says sodium ion chemistry is a good fit for commercial energy storage, but not necessarily EVs, because it prioritizes “longevity, high cycle and calendar life, and intense cost-efficiency.” The automaker is also working with Redwood Materials to build energy storage out of US-manufactured batteries, as well as “second-life” EV packs from GM’s vehicles. For its EV batteries, GM is betting on lithium manganese-rich batteries, or LMR, to close the gap between the US and China.Lastly, GM announced Energy Pass, a new feature that will appear across its suite of mobile apps. Energy Pass allows Chevy, Cadillac, and GMC EV owners to find, start, and pay for charging across multiple third-party charging operators, including Tesla, Electrify America, and IONNA. (The company says it also plans to add EVgo and ChargePoint.) Owners can use now use their mobile app to find a charger, initiate a charging session, and pay for the charging without having to sign up for a separate account for each provider.PreviousNext1/9The lack of convenient and reliable charging is frequently cited in customer surveys as a major obstacle to purchasing an EV. GM is using Tesla’s NACS charging standard for its future vehicles, as the company’s Superchargers are widely seen as among the best EV charging network in the world.GM has recently been trying to expand its EV business to include a variety of energy storage and charging projects. The company launched GM Energy, its energy spinoff, in 2022 as a way to compete in the rapidly expanding home energy market. In an effort to compete with Tesla for the $150 billion home energy market, GM sells a number of products, including home EV chargers, stationary home batteries, and vehicle-to-home (V2H) kits that enable a home to pull energy from an EV battery in the event of a blackout.Images courtesy of GM

history.titleZh: 通用汽车认为电动汽车可通过车网互联技术帮助抵消人工智能的能源消耗
history.descriptionZh:
在旧金山的一场活动上，通用汽车（GM）今日围绕电动汽车电池、能源存储以及电网韧性发布了一系列重要声明，旨在应对人工智能数据中心日益增长的电力需求。这家汽车制造商宣布，将为其现有的电动汽车和家庭能源客户激活新的车辆到电网（V2G）功能；同时推出以新开发的钠离子电池为核心的商用储能系统战略，用于工业级电网应用；此外，还面向电动汽车车主发布了一项新功能，旨在简化公共充电过程。

目前，全美有数百万辆电动汽车闲置在车道上，其电池中存储着大量电能。通用汽车押注，即使电动汽车销售降温，公共事业公司仍会愿意与汽车制造商合作，将这些电动汽车电池作为应对能源需求危机的潜在解决方案。这也是这家北美最大汽车制造商近四年来一直在尝试的、在数十亿美元的发电和储能市场中分一杯羹的最新努力。

“我们展望这样一个未来：电动汽车、驱动它们的电池，以及国家的电网能够协同工作，”通用汽车首席产品官斯特林·安德森在活动准备的发言稿中表示。

电动汽车的独特之处在于，它们既能从电网获取电能充电，也能将电能回馈给电网。许多电动汽车都具备这种双向充电能力，可实现能量的双向流动。本质上，它将高容量的锂离子电池不仅视为驱动电动汽车的工具，还当作备用储能单元，用于为其他电子设备充电、为整个家庭供电，甚至向电网输送电能，从而实现可能的节能效果。

随着AI数据中心给电网带来更大压力，通用汽车认为其数百万辆电动汽车可以帮助减轻负担。该公司表示，凭借双向充电能力，电动汽车可以在用电高峰时段将电能回馈电网。因此，通用汽车将发布固件更新，使其现有车辆到家庭（V2H）系统客户能够实现向电网输电（即车辆到电网，V2G）。已经拥有相关设备的通用汽车客户将自动获得此次更新。

通用汽车表示，目前美国道路上已有超过25万辆具备双向充电能力的雪佛兰、凯迪拉克和GMC电动汽车。理论上，它们合并的电池容量足以为12万户家庭连续供电一整周。

通用汽车已在两个州测试这一理论。在加州北部，该公司正与太平洋天然气和电力公司（PG&E）合作，开发一个由5.2万辆电动汽车组成的本地化车队，用于“电网平衡协议”，预计2030年投入运营。在密歇根州，通用汽车正与DTE Energy合作，利用其30名员工家庭作为真实测试案例，对双向充电进行“压力测试”。该公司表示，除了为公共事业公司带来好处外，电动汽车车主也可能获得经济收益。

“通过向历史上僵化的系统注入灵活性，V2G技术可以同时降低总能源成本，为消费者创造潜在的经济回报，并增强更广泛电网的系统可靠性，”安德森说。

然而，启用V2G技术并非易事。通用汽车能源业务副总裁韦德·谢弗在一封公开信中敦促监管机构将V2G基础设施正式化，并引用国际能源署（IEA）的报告指出，V2G是每小时灵活性最大的技术，能够限制未来电网投资成本。谢弗表示，汽车行业需要与政府合作，向公众普及V2G技术的好处，而公共事业公司必须简化行政流程，让客户能够无缝参与未来的项目。

通用汽车还表示，正在开发新的工业级解决方案，与加州初创公司Peak Energy合作，开发和部署钠离子化学储能系统。一些人认为钠比锂更优越，无论是在可用性还是稳定性方面。钠材料更经济实惠，且不像锂那样存在某些安全风险（锂在某些条件下可能起火）。钠离子电池在寒冷天气下的表现也优于锂离子电池。一些主要电池制造商，如中国的宁德时代，认为钠离子电池有可能取代目前主导市场的磷酸铁锂（LFP）电池高达一半的市场份额。

通用汽车表示，钠离子化学适用于商业储能，但不一定适用于电动汽车，因为它优先考虑“长寿命、高循环寿命和日历寿命，以及极致成本效益”。该公司还与Redwood Materials合作，利用美国制造的电池以及通用汽车车辆中的“二手”电池组来构建储能系统。对于电动汽车电池，通用汽车正押注于锂锰富集电池（LMR），以缩小美国与中国之间的差距。

最后，通用汽车宣布推出Energy Pass，这是一项将出现在其移动应用套件中的新功能。Energy Pass允许雪佛兰、凯迪拉克和GMC电动汽车车主在包括特斯拉、Electrify America和IONNA在内的多个第三方充电运营商处查找、启动和支付充电费用（该公司还计划添加EVgo和ChargePoint）。车主现在可以使用手机应用查找充电桩、启动充电会话并支付费用，无需为每个供应商单独注册账户。

便利性和可靠充电的缺乏经常在客户调查中被列为购买电动汽车的主要障碍。通用汽车未来车辆将采用特斯拉的NACS充电标准，因为特斯拉的超级充电网络被广泛认为是全球最好的电动汽车充电网络之一。

通用汽车最近一直试图扩展其电动汽车业务，涵盖各种储能和充电项目。该公司于2022年成立了能源子公司GM Energy，作为在快速扩张的家庭能源市场中竞争的方式。为了与特斯拉争夺1500亿美元的家庭能源市场，通用汽车销售多种产品，包括家用电动汽车充电器、固定式家用电池以及车辆到家庭（V2H）套件，后者允许家庭在停电时从电动汽车电池中取电。
